title: rydbergia grandiflora
intro: Rydbergia grandiflora, also known as Elephant's Head or Elephant's Head Clover, is a type of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae) native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Rydbergia grandiflora include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support digestive health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a salve or tincture, and can also be used as a tea to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
